title: 1.1: Reports Case A post-DATA SMTP response line is `550 5.7.23 SPF authentication failed (msg ID = <id>)`
1.2: Reports Case B post-DATA SMTP response line is `250 2.0.0 OK: queued`
1.3: Reports Case C post-DATA SMTP response line is `550 5.7.23 SPF authentication failed (msg ID = <id>)` (or the same line with a concrete msg ID)
1.4: Includes a Case A server log line showing the message was rejected at `DATA` due to `apply_spf` (contains `smtp: DATA error` and `"check":"apply_spf"`)
1.5: Includes a Case B server log line indicating SPF enforcement was deferred due to a DMARC policy (contains `deferring action due to a DMARC policy`)
1.6: Includes a Case B server log line showing SPF failed but no reject/quarantine action was taken (contains `smtp/pipeline: no check action` and `"check":"apply_spf"`)
1.7: Includes a Case C server log line showing the message was rejected at `DATA` due to `apply_spf` (contains `smtp: DATA error` and `"check":"apply_spf"`)
1.8: Identifies the deferral trigger condition as 'a DMARC record exists and the effective DMARC policy is not `none`'
1.9: Identifies why Case A does not defer as 'effective subdomain DMARC policy is `none` due to `sp=none` when using an organizational-domain record'
1.10: Identifies the runtime component that makes the deferral decision as `apply_spf`
1.11: States that the deferral decision is evaluated during end-of-`DATA` body processing (in `CheckBody`), not during `MAIL FROM` or `RCPT TO`
1.12: States that under deferral `apply_spf` clears enforcement by setting both `Reject=false` and `Quarantine=false` even on SPF failure
1.13: Explains why Case B can be accepted when no later pipeline component enforces DMARC after SPF deferral
1.14: Describes how to verify deferral vs enforcement using only SMTP responses and server logs (no repository modifications)
2.1: Claims the SPF check / deferral behavior belongs to a check name such as `check.spf`
2.2: Claims the `deferring action due to a DMARC policy` log line includes structured fields such as `err` and/or `result`
